---
title: "fdr_storey"
generated: true
description: "Storey's $ pi_0$-adaptive false-discovery-rate correction."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons`](../nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:fdr_storey -->
```python
fdr_storey(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, alpha: float = 0.05, lam: float = 0.5) -> Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:fdr_storey -->
Storey's $\pi_0$-adaptive false-discovery-rate correction.

Yields higher power, valid under independence or positive regression
dependence. This is Benjamini-Hochberg with the count $m$ replaced by
the estimated number of true nulls $m\,\pi_0$ (see
[`storey_pi0`](../nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons/storey_pi0.qmd)): uniformly less conservative than Benjamini-Hochberg when
a non-trivial fraction of effects are real, and identical to it when
$\pi_0 = 1$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:fdr_storey -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | — | Flat vector of `n` per-element p-values. |
| `alpha` | `float` | — | `0.05` | Target false-discovery rate defining the rejection mask. Default `0.05`. |
| `lam` | `float` | — | `0.5` | Tuning parameter $\lambda \in (0, 1)$ passed to [`storey_pi0`](../nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons/storey_pi0.qmd) for the $\pi_0$ estimate. Default `0.5`. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]`. The rejection mask `p_adjusted <= alpha`. The adaptive adjusted p-values (q-values), enforced monotone non-decreasing in rank.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:fdr_storey -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
